message:
audit(btree): ungated observability counters on 1t write hot path

Follow-up survey to d98e9e31 (cell-slot cache counter gating). Walked
every `AtomicU64::fetch_add` / `.store` call in
`fsqlite-btree/src/instrumentation.rs` to find counters that still fire
unconditionally on hot write paths.

Four counters remain ungated on the 1t write hot path:

- `record_operation` (every seek/insert/delete)
- `set_depth_gauge` (every btree op)
- `record_no_split_reuse_hit` (every in-place leaf insert — HOT)
- `record_page_header_rebuild` (every `reload_page_fresh` — HOT)

All four are single `Relaxed` atomic writes. d98e9e31's
`bench_cell_slot_cache_get_gate_on_vs_off` measured 0.036 ns per
counter on the same hardware — so the current 1t budget is bounded at
≤ 4 ns per op for these four.

Gating any of the four is NOT a win right now:

1. `record_operation` + `set_depth_gauge` + `record_split_event` are
   public via `btree_metrics_snapshot`. Gating would change external
   observer contract.

2. `record_no_split_reuse_hit` + `record_page_header_rebuild` +
   `record_conservative_reload_fallback` are asserted by ~10 leaf-reuse
   cursor tests plus `fsqlite-core/tests/fast_path_separation.rs`
   without flipping the copy-profile flag. Gating requires updating
   every one of those assertions.

Ship threshold for a further gating commit in this family: ≥ 2 ns
measured delta on a representative 1t bench OR a cross-core
cache-line-contention signal on an MT profile. Neither is present in
current profiles.

Full counter inventory + recommendation attached as bead comment
(bd-193kw). No code change; this is the explicit findings commit the
task asked for.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
